contract.name: v2/disaster/recipient/count.md
contract.description: Counts distinct recipients receiving disaster or emergency funding that satisfy the provided filters.

filter.description: Filter object controlling which disaster/emergency recipients are counted.
filter.constraints: Required. Must include def_codes array with at least one allowed DEF code. Additional keys are tolerated; only award_type_codes changed counts in probes.

def_codes.description: List of Disaster Emergency Fund codes to include in the aggregation.
def_codes.constraints: Required. Minimum 1 item. Each entry must exactly match one of ['1', '2', '3', '4', '5', '6', '7', '8', '9', 'A', 'AAA', 'AAB', 'AAC', 'AAD', 'AAE', 'AAF', 'AAG', 'AAH', 'AAI', 'AAJ', 'AAK', 'AAL', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'E', 'F', 'G', 'H', 'I', 'J', 'K', 'L', 'M', 'N', 'O', 'P', 'Q', 'QQQ', 'R', 'S', 'T', 'U', 'V', 'W', 'X', 'Y', 'Z']. Lowercase and whitespace variants are rejected (HTTP 400).

award_type_codes.description: Optional filter restricting results to specific procurement or assistance award types.
award_type_codes.constraints: Optional. When provided, must contain at least one value selected from ['-1','02','03','04','05','06','07','08','09','10','11','A','B','C','D','IDV_A','IDV_B','IDV_B_A','IDV_B_B','IDV_B_C','IDV_C','IDV_D','IDV_E']. Empty arrays and non-array payloads return validation errors.

time_period.description: Optional list of date range objects following the shared time_period filter schema.
time_period.constraints: Optional. Arrays of {start_date,end_date} objects are accepted, and even stray strings were tolerated; all observed payloads left the count unchanged, implying the filter is ignored.

recipient_scope.description: Optional shared filter indicating recipient scope (e.g., 'domestic', 'foreign').
recipient_scope.constraints: Optional. Accepted values behaved as no-ops in probes.

recipient_locations.description: Optional list of recipient location objects using the shared standard location shape.
recipient_locations.constraints: Optional. Accepted payloads left the count unchanged in probes.

keywords.description: Optional list of keyword strings from the shared filters set.
keywords.constraints: Optional. Accepted payloads did not affect the count in observed probes.

count.description: Number of unique recipients matching the applied filters.
count.constraints: Non-negative integer. Always present in successful responses.

quirks: Validation exposes a broad, uppercase-only DEF code list; lowercase or whitespace-variant codes are rejected with HTTP 400.
award_type_codes accepts procurement, assistance, and '-1' codes but enforces a non-empty array when present.
Shared filters such as time_period, recipient_scope, recipient_locations, and keywords were accepted yet produced no observable change in counts, implying they are ignored.
Unknown filter keys are silently ignored, while missing def_codes produces HTTP 422 and other validation issues return HTTP 400 with field-specific detail messages.
risks: Clients expecting shared filters (time_period, recipient_scope, recipient_locations, keywords) to narrow results may be misled because probes showed no effect.
Integrators must handle both 400 and 422 validation error shapes depending on the failure mode.
Relying on the enumerated DEF code list could break if USAspending updates the allowed values; prefer fetching the list dynamically.
gaps: Unclear whether additional shared filters (e.g., agencies, award amounts) are supported or ignored because they were not probed.
Impact of very large filter combinations on performance and response time remains untested.

probes.notes: pass - baseline multi-code request returned aggregate recipient count

body.detail: Missing value: 'filter|def_codes' is a required field

probes.notes: fail - body without filter rejected with 422 and explicit field path
fail - invalid DEFC value surfaces full enumeration list
pass - combining assistance award type codes reduced the recipient count
pass - time_period sent as a string was tolerated but left the count unchanged
pass - recipient_scope accepted but behaved as a no-op

mismatches: Docs restrict def_codes to CARES codes [L,M,N,O,P,U], but live validation accepts a much larger uppercase list including numeric and AAx codes.
Docs list award_type_codes without '-1', yet the API validator includes '-1' as an allowed value.
gaps: Unclear whether additional shared filters (e.g., agencies, award amounts) are supported or ignored because they were not probed.
Impact of very large filter combinations on performance and response time remains untested.
risks: Clients expecting shared filters (time_period, recipient_scope, recipient_locations, keywords) to narrow results may be misled because probes showed no effect.
Integrators must handle both 400 and 422 validation error shapes depending on the failure mode.
Relying on the enumerated DEF code list could break if USAspending updates the allowed values; prefer fetching the list dynamically.
